name: Peer Mailbox
summary: Agents communicate by writing per-message .md files into peer-specific inbox/replies folders on a shared filesystem. Pure file IO, no network or LLM in the transport, peer ids picked by senders. Distinct from MCP (RPC over a server) and inter-agent-communication (in-process messages).

raised_by.note: Sparrot's peer_messaging module exposes peer_send / peer_replies / list_peers over queue/peers/<peer>/inbox and queue/peers/<peer>/replies folders, one .md per message; atelier is one such peer among others.

rejected_reason: Subsumed as a file-system-based variant of actor-model-agents. The pattern's essential shape (per-actor mailbox, async messages, no shared mutable state) is already captured; '.md files in a shared filesystem' is a transport choice, not a new intent. Belongs in actor-model-agents.variants[] as a tactical variant if ever needed, not as a first-class pattern.
